# boundingBox(atTime:)

**Instance Method**

Returns the minimum region entirely enclosing the asset’s contents at the specified time sample.

## Declaration

```swift
func boundingBox(atTime time: TimeInterval) -> MDLAxisAlignedBoundingBox
```

## Parameters

- **time**: A timestamp referring to timed information in the asset.

## Return Value

The asset’s bounding box as of the specified time sample.

## Discussion

If an asset does not contain timed information, calling this method with any time sample is equivalent to reading the [doc://com.apple.modelio/documentation/ModelIO/MDLAsset/boundingBox] property.

All assets can provide bounding box information without traversing their hierarchy of objects.

## Working with Asset Content

- **object(at:)**: Returns the top-level object at the specified index in the asset.
- **subscript(_:)**: Returns the top-level object at the specified index in the asset, using subscript syntax.
- **count**: The number of top-level objects in the asset.
- **childObjects(of:)**: Returns all objects contained in the asset of the specified class.
- **add(_:)**: Adds the specified object to the asset’s list of top-level objects.
- **remove(_:)**: Removes the specified object from the asset’s list of top-level objects.
- **boundingBox**: The minimum region entirely enclosing the asset’s contents.
- **url**: The URL from which the asset was loaded, if available.
- **bufferAllocator**: An object responsible for allocating mesh vertex data loaded from the asset.
- **vertexDescriptor**: The description of the vertex data format to be used for loading mesh data from the asset.
- **masters**: An array of objects that can be reused in the asset’s object hierarchy through instancing.
